summary: Top‑tier audience recording that perfectly captures a legendary 6/30/87 show, highlighted by soaring Scarlet Begonias, Fire on the Mountain, Estimated Prophet and a monster The Other One.
review: The tape is praised as one of the best audience recordings from the summer‑’87 tour – crisp, well‑balanced and full of Healy’s signature echoes.  Musically the concert is described as an “above‑average” powerhouse with every member locked in, standout moments on Scarlet Begonias → Fire, a vocally spectacular Estimated Prophet, and an expansive Other One that leaves listeners “forever grateful.”

ai_rating.rationale: Over a dozen detailed reviews are uniformly enthusiastic about both the performance and the audio, giving the show a near‑perfect musical rating (≈4.5) and the audience recording an excellent technical rating (≈4.0). Weighted average yields ~4.3.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp, well‑balanced AUD capture; sweet‑spot mic placement praised; Healy’s effects are clear; minor imperfections noted but considered part of the charm.

show_quality.setlist_flow: A long, building first set that sets up an explosive second set; seamless transitions (Scarlet → Fire, Estimated Prophet showcase) and a high‑energy encore that kept the crowd ecstatic.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Repeated thanks for Jerry’s vocals and presence; his singing is highlighted as a key emotional driver of the show.
band_member_comments.Phil: Phil’s bass work gets multiple shout‑outs, especially during Estimated Prophet and the closing “thanks” to the audience.
band_member_comments.Bob: Bob Weir’s rap on Good Lovin and solid rhythm/vocal contributions are praised.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keyboardist Brent (Healy) is lauded for his creative echo/delay work that shapes many songs, especially Estimated Prophet and the overall soundscape.
band_member_comments.Drums: The drum sound—described as a “motorcycle” mic effect and space‑filled playing—is noted as powerful and one of the best of the period.
